1→<resources>
     2→    <string name="app_name">Aurora</string>
     3→</resources>

1→package ai.wallpaper.aurora.ui.theme
     2→
     3→import android.app.Activity
     4→import android.os.Build
     5→import androidx.compose.foundation.isSystemInDarkTheme
     6→import androidx.compose.material3.MaterialTheme
     7→import androidx.compose.material3.darkColorScheme
     8→import androidx.compose.material3.dynamicDarkColorScheme
     9→import androidx.compose.material3.dynamicLightColorScheme
    10→import androidx.compose.material3.lightColorScheme
    11→import androidx.compose.runtime.Composable
    12→import androidx.compose.ui.platform.LocalContext
    13→
    14→private val DarkColorScheme = darkColorScheme(
    15→    primary = Purple80,
    16→    secondary = PurpleGrey80,
    17→    tertiary = Pink80
    18→)
    19→
    20→private val LightColorScheme = lightColorScheme(
    21→    primary = Purple40,
    22→    secondary = PurpleGrey40,
    23→    tertiary = Pink40
    24→
    25→    /* Other default colors to override
    26→    background = Color(0xFFFFFBFE),
    27→    surface = Color(0xFFFFFBFE),
    28→    onPrimary = Color.White,
    29→    onSecondary = Color.White,
    30→    onTertiary = Color.White,
    31→    onBackground = Color(0xFF1C1B1F),
    32→    onSurface = Color(0xFF1C1B1F),
    33→    */
    34→)
    35→
    36→@Composable
    37→fun AuroraTheme(
    38→    darkTheme: Boolean = isSystemInDarkTheme(),
    39→    // Dynamic color is available on Android 12+
    40→    dynamicColor: Boolean = true,
    41→    content: @Composable () -> Unit
    42→) {
    43→    val colorScheme = when {
    44→        dynamicColor && Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= Build.VERSION_CODES.S -> {
    45→            val context = LocalContext.current
    46→            if (darkTheme) dynamicDarkColorScheme(context) else dynamicLightColorScheme(context)
    47→        }
    48→
    49→        darkTheme -> DarkColorScheme
    50→        else -> LightColorScheme
    51→    }
    52→
    53→    MaterialTheme(
    54→        colorScheme = colorScheme,
    55→        typography = Typography,
    56→        content = content
    57→    )
    58→}

content.text:
I inspected the project, and the main-screen top area is not implemented yet beyond the default Compose template.

## Main UI entry points

- The app launches into [app/src/main/java/ai/wallpaper/aurora/MainActivity.kt](app/src/main/java/ai/wallpaper/aurora/MainActivity.kt).
- `MainActivity.onCreate()` is the only runtime UI entry point right now:
  - It calls `enableEdgeToEdge()`
  - It sets Compose content with `AuroraTheme`
  - It renders a `Scaffold`
  - Inside the `Scaffold` content slot, it calls `Greeting(...)`
- The launcher/activity wiring is in [app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml](app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml), where `.MainActivity` is the launcher activity.
- The app title resource already exists as `"Aurora"` in [app/src/main/res/values/strings.xml](app/src/main/res/values/strings.xml#L2).

## Relevant composables and functions

### 1. `MainActivity.onCreate`
File: [app/src/main/java/ai/wallpaper/aurora/MainActivity.kt](app/src/main/java/ai/wallpaper/aurora/MainActivity.kt#L16-L31)

This is the current top-level Compose tree:

- `AuroraTheme { ... }`
- `Scaffold(modifier = Modifier.fillMaxSize()) { innerPadding -> ... }`
- `Greeting(name = "Android", modifier = Modifier.padding(innerPadding))`

This means the screen currently has:
- a Material 3 scaffold container
- no `topBar`
- no navigation icon
- no app bar title
- no menu button
- no drawer/menu implementation

### 2. `Greeting`
File: [app/src/main/java/ai/wallpaper/aurora/MainActivity.kt](app/src/main/java/ai/wallpaper/aurora/MainActivity.kt#L33-L39)

`Greeting` is currently just:

- a single `Text("Hello Android!")`

So there is no existing left-top menu button or any surrounding top composable structure to trace. The top-left area is effectively unused by custom UI.

### 3. `GreetingPreview`
File: [app/src/main/java/ai/wallpaper/aurora/MainActivity.kt](app/src/main/java/ai/wallpaper/aurora/MainActivity.kt#L41-L47)

Only a preview for the placeholder content.

## Toolbar / menu button placement findings

There is currently no toolbar/app bar composable in the codebase.

Specifically, I found no existing implementation of:
- `TopAppBar`
- `CenterAlignedTopAppBar`
- `SmallTopAppBar`
- `IconButton`
- `NavigationIcon`
- `DropdownMenu`
- `ModalNavigationDrawer`
- XML toolbar/menu resources for the main screen

The current `Scaffold` is being used only for layout padding, not for a top app bar.

## Best insertion point for a new icon + title text "Aurora"

The best insertion point is the `topBar` parameter of the existing `Scaffold` inside `MainActivity.onCreate()`.

That location is here:
- [app/src/main/java/ai/wallpaper/aurora/MainActivity.kt#L21-L27](app/src/main/java/ai/wallpaper/aurora/MainActivity.kt#L21-L27)

Why this is the best place:
- It is already the root Material 3 screen container.
- A top-left menu button belongs naturally in a `TopAppBar` `navigationIcon`.
- The app title `"Aurora"` belongs naturally in the app bar `title`.
- If you also want a leading app icon beside the title, it should go in the same top bar title row, not inside `Greeting`.

## Where the left-top menu button would live

If you add a Material 3 top app bar, the left-top menu button should be placed in the app bar’s `navigationIcon` slot.

Conceptually, the structure would be:

- `Scaffold(topBar = { ... })`
  - `TopAppBar(...)`
    - `navigationIcon = { IconButton(...) }`
    - `title = { Row { Icon(...); Text("Aurora") } }`

So the composables “around the left-top menu button” do not exist yet; they would need to be introduced around the existing `Scaffold`.

## Most relevant file for this work

The single most important file is:

- [app/src/main/java/ai/wallpaper/aurora/MainActivity.kt](app/src/main/java/ai/wallpaper/aurora/MainActivity.kt)

Supporting files:
- [app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml](app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml)
- [app/src/main/res/values/strings.xml](app/src/main/res/values/strings.xml)
- [app/src/main/java/ai/wallpaper/aurora/ui/theme/Theme.kt](app/src/main/java/ai/wallpaper/aurora/ui/theme/Theme.kt)

## Bottom line

Right now the app is still the default Compose starter screen. There is no implemented main-screen top bar or top-left menu button yet.

Best insertion point:
- add a `topBar` to the existing `Scaffold` in [app/src/main/java/ai/wallpaper/aurora/MainActivity.kt#L21-L27](app/src/main/java/ai/wallpaper/aurora/MainActivity.kt#L21-L27)

Best place for the new UI:
- menu button: app bar `navigationIcon`
- icon + `"Aurora"` title: app bar `title` content, likely as a `Row`

If you want, I can next outline the exact Compose structure to add there without editing files.
